logistics.explanation: Registration opens October 15, 2025, via ENDU.net with grids from Cesana Torinese at 08:00 for the 08:30 start in Sestriere.[1] Last-minute bibs available July 3-5 at 75€, limited to 300, ensuring accessible access near Turin Olympic sites.[1] Pasta party at Palazzetto dello Sport post-race simplifies recovery in upper Valle di Susa.[1]

length.explanation: Granfondo covers 103.6 km from Cesana Torinese via Oulx, Salbertrand to Colle delle Finestre summit.[1] Mediofondo shortens to 79 km ending in Sestriere after Pragelato descent.[1] Extended Granfondo adds Sauze di Cesana climb back to Sestriere finish.[1]

technicality.explanation: Last 8 km of Colle delle Finestre features gravel manageable on road bikes from Meana di Susa.[4] Route demands precise handling on unpaved summit amid Giro d'Italia heritage.[4] Paved sections through Exilles, Chiomonte, Gravere test sustained power before gravel.[1]

elevation.explanation: Granfondo packs 3170m gain over 103.6 km, peaking at Colle delle Finestre gravel summit.[1] Additional climbs to Pragelato, Sestriere, and final Cesana ascent amplify suffering.[1] Mediofondo's 2370m still challenges with same Finestre centerpiece.[1]

climate.explanation: July 5 event in Piedmont Alps promises warm starts but cooling highs at Sestriere.[1] High-altitude passages through Susa Valley risk variable mountain weather, yet proceeds under any conditions.[1] Olympic Valley di Susa microclimate suits summer gran fondos.[4]

altitude.explanation: Colle delle Finestre summit tests at over 2100m amid Turin Olympic mountains.[4] Sestriere finish at 2035m follows Pragelato and Cesana climbs in thin air.[1] Route from Cesana via Meana di Susa demands acclimation for prolonged hypoxia.[1]

adventure.explanation: Legendary Colle delle Finestre gravel evokes Chris Froome's 2018 Giro solo from this climb.[4] Piedmont parcours links Balboutet, Pourrieres, Usseaux in remote Chisone Valley.[1] Dual routes allow mid-race choice between 103.6 km granfondo or 79 km mediofondo.[1]

prestige.explanation: Fixed prestige leverages Giro d'Italia Colle delle Finestre history in Sestriere, epicenter of pro cycling.[3] XI edition draws 2500 to upper Valle di Susa roads of 2006 Turin Olympics.[1] Competitive categories award top 5 per group across granfondo and mediofondo.[1]

race_quality.explanation: Agonistic event with time gates at Sestriere by 13:30 and 7-hour max cutoff.[1] Categories by age/gender like Donne B (40-49) ensure fair regional competition.[2] Assistance and checkpoints enforce rules on Susa to Finestre parcours.[1]

experience.explanation: Pedal Giro heritage on unpaved Finestre from Meana di Susa to Pourrieres descent.[4] Celebrative maglia gadget and Piazza Fraiteve finish in Sestriere amplify immersion.[1] Pasta party post-12:00 arrivals celebrates Valle di Susa conquest.[1]

community.explanation: 2500 riders fill grids from red (gold sub) to white (2401-3000) fostering camaraderie.[1] Local Sestriere Bike committee hosts with Proloco bib pickup July 3-5.[1] Shared Giro legacy unites field on Pragelato-Sestriere finale.[3]

field_depth.explanation: Typical 2500 participants across 3000 bibs via layered grids in Cesana Torinese.[1] Age/gender splits like Uomini A, Donne C draw competitive regional depth.[2] Gold-limited first grid elevates front-pack intensity on Oulx approach.[1]

value.explanation: Early online fees via ENDU.net offer maglia and grids cheaper than 75€ last-minute.[1] Strava-worthy Finestre gravel justifies cost for 3170m in Piedmont Alps.[4] Awards top 5 per category add incentive without lottery barriers.[1]

expenses.explanation: Registrations rise to 75€ on-site July 3-5 in Sestriere, plus gold sub premium.[1] Alpine lodging near Cesana Torinese inflates costs for Olympic Valley stay.[4] Bike transport to remote Susa Valley adds to overall outlay.[1]

cultural_impact.explanation: Sestriere's pro cycling lore from Giro stages resonates nationally via Finestre fame.[3] XI edition cements status in Italy's gran fondo scene with 2500 locals/internationals.[3] Turin 2006 Olympic roads via Pragelato boost domestic prestige.[4]

course_description.summary: The Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre challenges riders with 103.6 km and 3170m elevation from Cesana Torinese, featuring the legendary gravel-topped Colle delle Finestre climb immortalized in Giro d'Italia history. The route winds through upper Valle di Susa and Chisone valleys via Oulx, Salbertrand, Exilles, Chiomonte, Gravere, Susa, and Meana di Susa before summiting Finestre and descending to Usseaux, Pourrieres, Pragelato, Sestriere (mediofondo end), then Sauze di Cesana for the granfondo finale. A shorter 79 km mediofondo shares the core ascent, allowing flexible route choice mid-event.

key_segments.name: Colle delle Finestre
key_segments.description: Iconic climb from Susa via Meana di Susa, last 8 km gravel to summit.

key_segments.name: Sestriere Finale
key_segments.description: Climb through Cesana from Sauze di Cesana after Pragelato descent.

key_segments.name: Pragelato-Sestriere
key_segments.description: Rolling ascent post-Finestre via Pourrieres and Balboutet.

course_description.elevation_profile: Rolling start from Cesana Torinese builds through Susa Valley to brutal Colle delle Finestre ramp (2178m peak), sharp gravel descent, then punchy climbs to Sestriere (2035m) with late Cesana kicker.
course_description.aid_stations: Assistance at checkpoints; pasta party post-race at Sestriere Palazzetto dello Sport.

travel.driving_from_major_city: 2h from Turin via A32 to Susa Valley
travel.accommodation: Sestriere, Cesana Torinese hotels; Olympic Valley options
travel.bike_transport: Fly to TRN; drive or shuttle to Cesana

race_day.categories: Age/gender: Uomini A/B/C, Donne A/B/C; top 5 per gran/medio
race_day.awards: Prizes for top 5 per category, non-cumulative

training_recommend.ideal_preparation: 8-12 weeks building to 100k+ with 3000m+, gravel sims
training_recommend.key_workouts:
- 3x20min @ threshold
- Finestre sim: 2h steady + 10km gravel steep
- Back-to-back 4000m days

history.notable_facts: XI edition 2026; sole gran fondo worldwide with full Colle delle Finestre gravel; Chris Froome's 2018 Giro-winning solo launched here; Sestriere Bike Grandi Eventi hosts alongside gravel/MTB events.

final_verdict.one_liner: Giro legend gravel assault in Olympic Piedmont: suffer Finestre, conquer Sestriere.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Climbers craving 3170m gran fondo with storied unpaved HC; roadies fit for 7h Alps.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Gravel novices or sub-3000m trainers; prefer flats over 2000m+ hypoxia.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Opt gold sub for red grid; scout Finestre gravel on road bike Sat gravel event for €90 package.

suffering_zones.label: Finestre Gravel Death
suffering_zones.description: Steep unpaved ramps above Meana di Susa shred legs at 2100m+; Froome's shadow looms.

suffering_zones.label: Cesana Kicker
suffering_zones.description: Post-Sauze di Cesana wall to Sestriere finish; crowds roar after 100km fatigue.

suffering_zones.label: Susa Valley Build
suffering_zones.description: False flat grind via Exilles-Chiomonte primes for Finestre; deceptive early burn.
